description:
Legal record (#12) in a court register/pinqas from a beit din in Cairo (on f. 1r-v of T-S K22.27). In Hebrew with some terms in Judaeo-Arabic. Location: Cairo. Dated in the middle third of Adar I 5453, which is 1693 CE. 

(Document #12) Real estate case with three judges' signatures regarding an endowment property (waqfiyya), specifically a commercial house (אוכאלה=وكالة) in the "port of Damietta" that has fallen into ruin. The entry is based on witness testimony about the state of the property whose waqf status is explained in the opening line of the entry (וקפייאת הקרקעות שעשה הרב הכולל מוהרי׳ קשטרו זלה׳׳ה)– pointing to the founder of the endowment as R. Castro, but with no first name included among the honorifics. The description of the property mentions the collapse of various walls and the lack of a door/lock on the building (דלת ובריח אין לה). The document entry also discusses arrangements for future repairs, ground rent (arḍiyya), and for the property's succession among current/future descendants of the Castro family. The full name of the commercial house appears later in line 35 (1r) and line 16 (1v): Wikālat al-Mhāzqa/Mrāzqa (the exact spelling of the title requires further research). MCD.
description_he: רשומה משפטית (#12) בפנקס בית דין בקהיר (T-S K22.27, דפים 1r-v); בעברית עם מונחים יהודית-ערבית; אדר א' ה'תנ"ג (1693 לספירה); תיק נדל"ן עם חתימות שלושה שופטים בנוגע לנכס וַקְף - אוּכָאלָה (בית מסחר) ב'נמל דמיאט' שנפל לאבדן; הוקדש על ידי ר' קסטרו; מתאר קריסת קירות, חוסר דלת ותכניות לשיפוץ ולוויסות ירושת בני משפחת קסטרו
